channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Congo and Rwanda Compete for Leadership of Francophone Organisation Amid Eastern Conflict
item.description: Congo and Rwanda are set to clash diplomatically over the leadership of the International Organisation of La Francophonie (OIF). Both countries have presented candidates for the secretary general posi...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh High Court declares Bhojshala as a Saraswati temple, Hindu worship to begin
item.description: On Friday, the Indore bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court delivered a landmark verdict regarding the disputed Bhojshala Temple-Kamal Maula Mosque complex in Dhar district. The court recognised the ...

item.title: How to Navigate Disliking Your Friend’s Partner While Preserving Your Friendship
item.description: Disliking a friend’s partner is a common yet tricky situation. Experts advise first reflecting on your own feelings: sometimes jealousy, fear of losing the friend, or personal insecurities may amplify...

item.title: U.S. and Nigerian Forces Kill ISIS Leader Abu-Bilal al-Minuki in Joint Operation
item.description: Former President Donald Trump announced that U.S. and Nigerian forces successfully executed a joint operation that killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, whom he described as the second-in-command of ISIS global...

item.title: Joan Crawford’s Evolution Through Movie Magazine Covers, 1920s–1950s
item.description: Joan Crawford was a prominent figure on movie magazine covers from the late 1920s to the 1950s, reflecting the changing styles and glamour of Hollywood. In the 1920s, she was often portrayed as the en...

item.title: Inside Kasuwar Alkaleri: Bauchi’s Historic Grain Trading Hub
item.description: Every Sunday morning, the town of Alkaleri in Bauchi State dey come alive as Kasuwar Alkaleri opens for business. Dis market, wey don dey for over 70 years, dey one of the most important places wey gr...

item.title: JOOUST Student Alice Riang’a Murdered; Family, Boyfriend Mourn Her Death
item.description: The nation was left in shock after Alice Riang’a, a second-year student at Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology (JOOUST), was found murdered in Siaya County. Her decomposing bod...

item.title: SinoAm Global Fund Plans USD 5 Billion Investment in Tanzania Infrastructure via PPP
item.description: Sino American Global Fund, a US-based investment firm, has shown readiness to invest up to USD 5 billion in Tanzania through public-private partnership (PPP) projects. The investment is planned to cov...

item.title: President Marcos Jr. reminds PMA graduates to uphold loyalty to the Constitution
item.description: During the Philippine Military Academy (PMA) graduation ceremonies, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. addressed the new officers, emphasizing the importance of loyalty to the Constitution above personal,...

item.title: Vivo Energy Launches Modern One-Stop Shell Service Station in Kilimani, Nairobi
item.description: Vivo Energy Kenya has officially opened a new Shell service station along Dennis Pritt Road in Kilimani, Nairobi, aimed at providing motorists with a comprehensive one-stop retail and service experien...

item.title: Jess Wilson aims to improve social media presence ahead of Victoria election
item.description: As Victoria gears up for the state election in November, Jess Wilson is intensifying efforts to enhance the Liberal Party's online presence, attempting to counter years of Labor dominance on social me...

item.title: Kerala Government Cabinet Allocations: Congress to Get 11 Ministries, IUML 5
item.description: Kerala chief minister-designate VD Satheesan has affirmed support for his ally, the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML), amid claims by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) that the IUML influenced his sele...

item.title: Karachi’s Experiment with Pavers: A Partial Fix to Persistent Road Damage
item.description: Karachi has been experimenting with paver blocks as a solution to chronic road damage caused by flooding and leaking sewerage lines. Areas like Hijri Road in Gulshan Town and the FTC flyover have seen...

item.title: Affordable Summer Wardrobe Essentials for Women
item.description: Jess Cartner-Morley provides a comprehensive guide to updating your summer wardrobe without exceeding £100 per item. The article emphasises simplicity in summer style, encouraging readers to rely on r...

item.title: Tow Truck Driver Dies in Workplace Accident in Brampton
item.description: A tragic workplace accident occurred in Brampton, Ontario, resulting in the death of a tow truck driver. According to Peel Regional Police, the incident happened when the individual became pinned unde...

item.title: President Tinubu returns to Lagos after official visits to France, Kenya, and Rwanda
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don return Lagos on Friday evening after him waka go three countries – France, Kenya, and Rwanda. Him plane land for the Presidential Wing of Murtala Muhammed International...

item.title: Events and Activities in Metro Vancouver for the May Long Weekend
item.description: This article provides a comprehensive guide to events and activities happening across Metro Vancouver during the May long weekend. Among the highlights are family-friendly attractions like Vancouver P...

item.title: Nigeria and Ghana to participate in 2026 Karibu-Kilifair as Tanzania boosts tourism
item.description: For the first time, Nigeria and Ghana will take part in the 2026 Karibu-Kilifair, an international tourism exhibition held in Arusha, Tanzania. The event, scheduled from June 4 to 6 at the Magereza Gr...

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo’s Ituri Province Claims 80 Lives Amid Ongoing Humanitarian Crisis
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak has emerged in the Ituri province of eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), resulting in 80 confirmed deaths, the Ministry of Health reported. Laboratory tests confirmed eigh...

item.title: Nigerian Banks' Lending Trends and Sector Focus in 2025
item.description: Over the past five years, Nigerian banks don sharply increase how dem dey lend money, but the way dem dey distribute am dey show where capital dey flow and where gaps still dey for financing across th...
